englishMeaning: Demonstrating aggression and willingness to fight.
mnemonics: Belli- (war) + cose. Think of 'Belly' full of 'War'.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'The nation's bellicose rhetoric alarmed its neighbors.'

englishMeaning: Kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit.
mnemonics: Plan-destine: A secret plan to reach a destination.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2019: 'The agency carried out a clandestine operation.'

englishMeaning: Courage in pain or adversity.
mnemonics: Fort-itude: Like a 'Fort', you stand strong against attacks.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2022: 'Soldiers show great fortitude during long deployments.'

englishMeaning: Disinterested and selfless concern for the well-being of others.
mnemonics: Al-tru-ism: All true people care for others.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2018: 'The General was known for his altruism toward veterans.'

englishMeaning: Lasting for a very short time.
mnemonics: E-phem-eral: Sounds like 'E-funeral' (life is short).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'Fame in the digital age is often ephemeral.'

englishMeaning: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
mnemonics: Girls-us: (Stereotype) Girls talk a lot.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'The garrulous neighbor kept us for hours.'

englishMeaning: Not able to be corrected, improved, or reformed.
mnemonics: In-correct-able: Someone who cannot be made correct.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'He is an incorrigible liar.'

englishMeaning: A lack of energy and enthusiasm.
mnemonics: Leth-argy: Sounds like 'Late-argy' (too lazy/late to move).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'Heavy meals often induce lethargy.'

englishMeaning: Generous or forgiving, especially toward a rival or less powerful person.
mnemonics: Magna- (Large) + Animous (Heart/Soul).
previousYearUsage: CDS 2017: 'The victor was magnanimous in defeat.'

englishMeaning: Obedient or attentive to an excessive or servile degree.
mnemonics: Obse- (Obsessed) + Quious (Quick to say yes).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'He had an obsequious manner that many found annoying.'

englishMeaning: Acting with or showing care and thought for the future.
mnemonics: P-student: A 'Proper' student is always careful.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2019: 'It is prudent to save money for emergencies.'

englishMeaning: In a state or period of inactivity or dormancy.
mnemonics: Quies-cent: Sounds like 'Quiet'.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'The volcano has been quiescent for centuries.'

englishMeaning: Not revealing one's thoughts or feelings readily.
mnemonics: Ready-silent: Someone who is always ready to stay silent.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'She was reticent about her personal life.'

englishMeaning: Optimistic or positive, especially in an apparently bad or difficult situation.
mnemonics: Sang-uine: Thinking of 'Penguins' singing makes you happy.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'He remains sanguine about the company's future.'

englishMeaning: Reserved or uncommunicative in speech; saying little.
mnemonics: Taxi-turn: Taxi drivers who don't talk turn away from you.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2018: 'The captain was a taciturn man.'

englishMeaning: Present, appearing, or found everywhere.
mnemonics: Ubi-quit-ous: 'U' (You) are 'bit' (everywhere) 'us'.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'Smartphones are ubiquitous in modern society.'

englishMeaning: Regard with great respect; revere.
mnemonics: When you 'rate' someone 'v' (very) high, you venerate them.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2019: 'We venerate our martyrs.'

englishMeaning: Feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems.
mnemonics: Wary: Beware + Scary (Be cautious of scary things).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'Dogs that have been mistreated are often wary of strangers.'

englishMeaning: A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their religious, political, or other ideals.
mnemonics: Zeal-lot: A person with a 'Lot' of 'Zeal' (energy).
previousYearUsage: NDA 2020: 'He was a religious zealot.'

englishMeaning: Restrain oneself from doing or enjoying something.
mnemonics: Abs-stain: To get 'Abs', you 'stain' (stay) away from junk food.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2023: 'She chose to abstain from voting.'

englishMeaning: Brisk and cheerful readiness.
mnemonics: Alacrity: Think of 'All-is-great-city'—everyone is eager to work.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'She accepted the invitation with alacrity.'

englishMeaning: Well-meaning and kindly.
mnemonics: Bene (Good) + Volent (Wishing). Wishing good for others.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2017: 'The king was known for his benevolent rule.'

englishMeaning: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior.
mnemonics: Caprice: Like 'Car-price' in India—it changes suddenly.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'A capricious climate makes farming difficult.'

englishMeaning: Humble submission and respect.
mnemonics: Defer-ence: If you 'Defer' your opinion to an elder, you show respect.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2018: 'He addressed the court with due deference.'

englishMeaning: Make a problem, bad situation, or negative feeling worse.
mnemonics: Ex-acerb-ate: Acerb (bitter) + ate. Making things more bitter.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'The heavy rain only exacerbated the traffic problem.'

englishMeaning: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail.
mnemonics: Fastidious: Someone who is 'fast' to get 'hideous' (angry) if things aren't perfect.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'Military officers are trained to be fastidious about their uniform.'

englishMeaning: Fond of company; sociable.
mnemonics: Gre- (Groups) + garious. Likes to live in groups.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'Dolphins are gregarious animals.'

englishMeaning: Arrogantly superior and disdainful.
mnemonics: Haughty: Sounds like 'Hotty'—someone who thinks they are too hot/cool for others.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2019: 'Her haughty attitude alienated her teammates.'

englishMeaning: Certain to happen; unavoidable.
mnemonics: In-every-table: Something that will reach every table eventually.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'Death is an inevitable part of life.'

englishMeaning: Having, showing, or done with good judgment or sense.
mnemonics: Judicious: Like a 'Judge'—takes wise decisions.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'The judicious use of resources is key to success.'

englishMeaning: Light or set on fire; arouse or inspire (an emotion).
mnemonics: Kindle: Like the 'Kindle' e-reader sparks an interest in reading.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2019: 'His speech kindled a sense of patriotism in the audience.'

englishMeaning: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative.
mnemonics: Loqua- (Talk) + cious. Root word 'Loqu' means talk.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'The loquacious host made everyone feel welcome.'

englishMeaning: Make less severe, serious, or painful.
mnemonics: Miti-gate: Think of 'Mitti' (soil) poured on a fire to make it less severe.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2023: 'Drainage systems help mitigate the effects of floods.'

englishMeaning: Wicked or criminal (typically of an action or activity).
mnemonics: Ne-fari-ous: Sounds like 'No-Fairy'—fairies are good, so no-fairy is bad/evil.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2017: 'The hacker had nefarious intentions.'

englishMeaning: Involving an amount of effort and difficulty that is oppressively burdensome.
mnemonics: Onerous: Sounds like 'On-us'—a heavy responsibility is 'on us'.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2018: 'He found the duties of the captaincy to be onerous.'

englishMeaning: Engaged in, involving, or reflecting deep or serious thought.
mnemonics: Pen-sive: When you hold a 'Pen' and wait to write, you are thinking deeply.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2022: 'She sat by the window in a pensive mood.'

englishMeaning: Put an end to (a rebellion or other disorder), typically by the use of force.
mnemonics: Quell: Sounds like 'Kill'—killing a rebellion to stop it.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'Troops were sent to quell the riot.'

englishMeaning: Strong and healthy; vigorous.
mnemonics: Robust: Sounds like 'Robot'—robots are built strong.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'The country needs a robust defense system.'

englishMeaning: Diligent, thorough, and extremely attentive to details; very concerned to avoid doing wrong.
mnemonics: Scrupulous: Think of 'Sculpture'—an artist is very detailed and careful with it.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'He is scrupulous in all his business dealings.'

englishMeaning: Lasting only for a short time; impermanent.
mnemonics: Transit: Like people in 'Transit' (at airport), they are there for a short time.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2019: 'The transient nature of youth.'

englishMeaning: Courteous, confident, and refined in manner.
mnemonics: Urbane: People living in 'Urban' cities are expected to be sophisticated.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'He was an urbane and witty companion.'

englishMeaning: Keeping careful watch for possible danger or difficulties.
mnemonics: Vigil-ant: Like a 'Vigilante' or a 'Watchman' (Vigil).
previousYearUsage: NDA 2020: 'Soldiers must remain vigilant at the borders.'

englishMeaning: Become unsteady or unreliable; be undecided between two opinions or courses of action.
mnemonics: Waver: Like a 'Wave' moving up and down—not steady.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'His resolve did not waver despite the threats.'

englishMeaning: The time at which something is most powerful or successful.
mnemonics: Zenith: Sounds like 'Z-height'—the maximum height.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2017: 'The Roman Empire reached its zenith in the 2nd century.'

englishMeaning: Self-restraining; not overindulging in food or drink.
mnemonics: Abstain + ious: Full of the quality of 'Abstaining'.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'His abstentious lifestyle contributed to his long life.'

englishMeaning: Truthful and straightforward; frank.
mnemonics: Candid: Think of 'Candid Camera'—it catches people being real/natural.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2018: 'I look forward to your candid opinion.'

englishMeaning: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work or duties.
mnemonics: Dili-gent: People from 'Delhi' are 'Gents' (diligent workers).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'A diligent student will always succeed.'

englishMeaning: A person or thing that is mysterious, puzzling, or difficult to understand.
mnemonics: Enigma: The famous 'Enigma Machine' used to send secret codes in WWII.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'His sudden disappearance remains an enigma.'

englishMeaning: Sparing or economical with regard to money or food.
mnemonics: Fru-gal: A girl who eats only 'Fruits' to save money.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'He led a frugal life to save for his daughter's education.'

englishMeaning: Easily persuaded to believe something; credulous.
mnemonics: Gull-ible: A 'Gull' (bird) is easily 'able' to be caught.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2023: 'Don't be so gullible as to believe everything you read.'

englishMeaning: A lengthy and aggressive speech.
mnemonics: Har-angue: Sounds like 'Her-Anger'. When she is angry, she gives a long speech.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'The protesters were energized by his harangue.'

englishMeaning: In accordance with the highest standards; faultless.
mnemonics: Im-peccable: 'Pec' sounds like 'Puncture' (flaw). Im-peccable = No punctures/flaws.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'Her English was impeccable.'

englishMeaning: For or relating to young people; childish.
mnemonics: Juvenile: Similar to 'Junior'.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2019: 'The judge dealt with the juvenile offender leniently.'

englishMeaning: An acquired or natural skill at performing a task.
mnemonics: Knack: Sounds like 'Neck'. You have the 'knack' to turn your neck and see everything.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'He has a knack for getting into trouble.'

englishMeaning: Displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion or effort.
mnemonics: Languid: Sounds like 'liquid'. A person moves like a slow-moving liquid.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'They spent a languid afternoon by the pool.'

englishMeaning: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise.
mnemonics: Meticulous: 'Meet-i-culous'. If you meet a meticulous person, they check every detail.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'The pilot performed a meticulous check of the aircraft.'

englishMeaning: The lowest point in the fortunes of a person or organization.
mnemonics: Nadir: Opposite of Zenith. Think of 'Na-gir' (Falling down).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2018: 'They had reached the nadir of their relationship.'

englishMeaning: Not able to be seen through; not transparent.
mnemonics: Opaque: Sounds like 'Pack'. If a box is packed, you can't see through it.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'The glass was painted to be opaque.'

englishMeaning: Eager or quick to argue, quarrel, or fight.
mnemonics: Pugnacious: Think of a 'Pug' dog that is always ready to bark/fight.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2023: 'His pugnacious nature made him many enemies.'

englishMeaning: Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.
mnemonics: Quixotic: Based on 'Don Quixote' who fought windmills thinking they were giants.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2019: 'The project was a quixotic effort to end poverty.'

englishMeaning: Say something again or a number of times, typically for emphasis or clarity.
mnemonics: Re-iterate: 'Re' (again) + Iterate (repeat).
previousYearUsage: NDA 2022: 'The commander reiterated the importance of discipline.'

englishMeaning: A person who acts obsequiously toward someone important in order to gain advantage.
mnemonics: Sycophant: 'Psycho' + 'Fan'. A fan who is psycho about a celebrity/boss.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'The CEO was surrounded by sycophants.'

englishMeaning: Tending to keep a firm hold of something; clinging or adhering closely.
mnemonics: Ten-acious: A person with the strength of 'Ten' people holding on.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'The defense held a tenacious grip on the lead.'

englishMeaning: Find fault with (someone); scold.
mnemonics: Up-braid: If you don't 'braid' your hair properly, your mother will scold you.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'The teacher upbraided the student for being late.'

englishMeaning: Alternate or waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.
mnemonics: Vacillate: Sounds like 'Oscillate' (moving back and forth).
previousYearUsage: CDS 2019: 'He vacillated between the two job offers.'

englishMeaning: Skilled at gaining an advantage, especially deceitfully.
mnemonics: Wily: Think of 'Wile E. Coyote' from Looney Tunes.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'The wily politician managed to evade the questions.'

englishMeaning: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries.
hindiMeaning: विदेशी लोगों से डर (Videshi logon se darr)
mnemonics: Xeno (Foreign) + Phobia (Fear).
previousYearUsage: CDS 2018: 'Xenophobia can lead to serious social unrest.'

englishMeaning: Produce or provide; give way to arguments, demands, or pressure.
hindiMeaning: पैदावार / हार मान लेना (Paidawar / Haar maan lena)
mnemonics: Yield: A 'Yield' sign on the road means give way to others.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2020: 'The land yielded a rich harvest.'

englishMeaning: Having or showing zeal; great energy or enthusiasm.
mnemonics: Zeal: Sounds like 'Seal' (a deal). You are excited to seal the deal.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'He is a zealous supporter of environmental reform.'

englishMeaning: A place of residence; a house or home.
mnemonics: Abode: A-board. You have a board with your name outside your house.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2017: 'Welcome to my humble abode.'

englishMeaning: So lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring.
mnemonics: Banal: Sounds like 'Ban all'. Ban all boring movies.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'The dialogue in the play was banal.'

englishMeaning: Persuade someone to do something by sustained coaxing or flattery.
mnemonics: Cajole: 'Kajol' (actress) tries to cajole the hero in movies.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'He hoped to cajole her into selling the house.'

englishMeaning: Ready to accept control or instruction; submissive.
mnemonics: Docile: Sounds like 'Dosa'. Making a dosa is easy; it follows the pan.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'The once wild horse is now quite docile.'

englishMeaning: Cause (someone) to feel drained of energy or vitality; weaken.
mnemonics: E-ner-vate: 'Energy' + 'Waste'. Your energy is wasted/gone.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'The heat of the sun enervated the travelers.'

englishMeaning: Understand (a difficult problem or an enigmatic person) after much thought.
mnemonics: Fathom: A unit of depth. To fathom is to get to the 'depth' of a matter.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'He couldn't fathom why she left.'

englishMeaning: Dwell on one's own success or another's misfortune with smugness.
mnemonics: Gloat: Sounds like 'Goat'. A goat bleating after winning a fight.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2018: 'Don't gloat over your rival's failure.'

englishMeaning: A pause or gap in a sequence, series, or process.
mnemonics: Hiatus: 'Hi' + 'At' + 'Us'. We said 'Hi' and then took a break.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'The band is on a hiatus for a year.'

englishMeaning: About to happen.
mnemonics: Imminent: 'Immediate'. Something immediate is imminent.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'The storm warning meant danger was imminent.'

englishMeaning: Cheerful and friendly.
mnemonics: Jovial: Sounds like 'Joyful'.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2023: 'He was in a jovial mood after the party.'

englishMeaning: One's family and relations; similar in kind or character.
mnemonics: Kindred: 'Kind' + 'Red'. Family members are the same 'kind' and share the same 'red' blood.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2019: 'They are kindred spirits.'

englishMeaning: Expressed clearly; easy to understand.
mnemonics: Lucid: Sounds like 'Liquid'. A clear liquid like water.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'The witness gave a lucid account of the event.'

englishMeaning: Lacking interest or excitement; dull.
mnemonics: Mundane: Monday + ane. Mondays are always dull/ordinary.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2022: 'The mundane tasks of daily life.'

englishMeaning: Feeling or appearing casually calm and relaxed; not displaying anxiety.
mnemonics: Nonchalant: 'No' + 'Chalan'. If you have no chalan (fine) to pay, you are relaxed.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'She gave a nonchalant shrug.'

englishMeaning: Destroy utterly; wipe out.
mnemonics: Obliterate: 'O' + 'Blue' + 'Iterate'. Use blue ink to rub out/repeat over a mistake.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2018: 'The memory was obliterated by the trauma.'

englishMeaning: A seemingly absurd or self-contradictory statement or proposition that when investigated may prove to be true.
mnemonics: Paradox: Two 'dox' (dogs) fighting each other but are actually brothers.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'It is a paradox that less is more.'

englishMeaning: Put an end to (a rebellion or other disorder), typically by the use of force.
mnemonics: Quell: Sounds like 'Quiet'. To make a riot quiet.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2023: 'The police were called to quell the disturbance.'

englishMeaning: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions.
mnemonics: Resilient: Like an 'Eraser'—you press it, it comes back to shape.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2022: 'He is a resilient person who never gives up.'

englishMeaning: Extremely dirty and unpleasant, especially as a result of poverty or neglect.
mnemonics: Squalid: Sounds like 'Squid'. A squid in dirty water.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2019: 'They lived in squalid conditions.'

englishMeaning: Equivalent in seriousness to; virtually the same as.
mnemonics: Tant-a-mount: 'Ta-na-na' (same tune) + 'Amount'. Same amount.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2020: 'His refusal to answer was tantamount to an admission of guilt.'

englishMeaning: Lacking good manners, refinement, or grace.
mnemonics: Un-couth: 'Un' (Not) + 'Youth' (civilized). Not civilized.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2021: 'His uncouth behavior shocked the guests.'

englishMeaning: Clear (someone) of blame or suspicion.
mnemonics: Vin-dicate: 'Win' + 'the' + 'case'.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2020: 'The new evidence will vindicate him.'

englishMeaning: Become unsteady or unreliable; be undecided between two opinions.
mnemonics: Waver: Similar to 'Wave'. Moving like a wave.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2021: 'His voice wavered with emotion.'

englishMeaning: Angry and bitter (typically of a speech or a debate).
mnemonics: Acri-monious: 'Acrid' (bitter smell). A bitter relationship.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2019: 'The divorce was acrimonious.'

englishMeaning: Hostile and aggressive.
mnemonics: Belli- (War) + Gerent. Always ready for war.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2022: 'The country's belligerent attitude led to war.'

englishMeaning: Kept secret or done secretively, especially because illicit.
mnemonics: Clan-destine: A 'Clan' meeting at a secret 'Destination'.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2018: 'The group held clandestine meetings in the basement.'

englishMeaning: Having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work.
mnemonics: Diligent: 'Delhi' + 'Gents' are hardworking.
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2023: 'She is a diligent worker.'

englishMeaning: Cheerful and full of energy.
mnemonics: Ebullient: Sounds like 'Boiling' (energy bubbling over).
previousYearUsage: NDA 2023: 'He was ebullient after his victory.'

englishMeaning: Very attentive to and concerned about accuracy and detail.
mnemonics: Fastidious: A person who is 'fast' to get 'hideous' results if not perfect.
previousYearUsage: CDS 2022: 'He was fastidious about his appearance.'

englishMeaning: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters.
mnemonics: Garrulous: Sounds like 'Girls' + 'Rule' (talk a lot).
previousYearUsage: AFCAT 2020: 'The garrulous old man told us his life story.'

englishMeaning: Arrogantly superior and disdainful.
mnemonics: Haughty: 'Hot' + 'Tea'. Only rich/arrogant people drink expensive hot tea with a pinky finger up.
previousYearUsage: NDA 2021: 'The haughty waitress ignored us.'
